key_insight: LoadKAN combines feature-isolated temporal attention with Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks to produce accurate electricity load forecasts while revealing interpretable feature-specific nonlinear relationships.
engineering_relevance: The method offers grid operators a forecasting model that is both competitive with black-box deep learning systems and more transparent for understanding demand drivers such as mobility patterns.
method: Isolate each input feature and apply temporal attention separately to capture its own dynamics.
Fuse the distilled feature representations with a KAN module for interpretable nonlinear forecasting.
Analyze learned activation functions and sensitivity to quantify how mobility features affect load.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on datasets from three representative U.S. electricity markets and remained highly competitive against extensively tuned state-of-the-art deep learning benchmarks.
watch_item: The feature-isolated attention design and KAN interpretability may add modeling complexity and require careful tuning to avoid losing temporal interactions across features.
paper_title: Interpretable Kolmogorov-Arnold Network with Feature-Isolated Temporal Attention Mechanism for Electricity Load Forecasting
